I'm reposting this for awareness: User Clear-Corridor has amassed a staggering 150,362 YES shares (over $36,000 in capital). This is not someone who genuinely believes Hezbollah signed a ceasefire today; this is a hostile oracle takeover attempt.

Here is their playbook:

Buy up cheap "YES" shares while the fundamental reality points to "NO".

Wait for the market to resolve "NO" at 11:59 PM ET tonight.

Immediately Dispute the resolution, forcing it to the UMA Oracle.

Spam the UMA voter channels with the April 23rd Trump headlines ("Ceasefire Extended by 3 Weeks!") and try to scam the token-holders into voting YES based on the Lebanon technicality.
They'll lose anyway, as they lost with the US-Iran ceasefire agreement (same implicit arguments). The only question is whether they only lose money or also time and reputation.

Thanks, i think it doesn't matter what did say Trump. Becouse after he announced a three-week extension of the ceasefire on April 23, Hezbollah issued a sharp rebuke via its Al-Manar TV channel.

Hezbollah MP Ali Fayyad called the truce 'meaningless' as long as Israeli troops remain on Lebanese soil.

The group stated that it reserves the right to 'respond proportionately' to any Israeli actions, effectively disavowing the ceasefire commitment.

According to the rules: "An extension... requires clear public confirmation from both the Israeli government and Hezbollah..."

Since Hezbollah:
– Has not officially confirmed its consent to the extension.
– Has continued its shelling (e.g., the rocket barrage on Shula on April 23, launched during the negotiations).
– Has publicly labeled the agreement as invalid regarding their own actions.
– Has publicly clearly and resolutely declares that continuation of resistance directed at Israeli military targets. – 26.04.2026 ❗️

As of now, this qualifies as a "NO" outcome.

The first paragraph already disqualifies everything. It mandates that Hezbollah's mutual agreement is required, 'Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No" '.

Hezbollah obviously hasn't mutually agreed. In fact, they openly rejected it, calling it meaningless and media reports clearly reflect that reality, so NO.

`This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the 10-day ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah announced on April 16, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between Israel and Hezbollah, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".`

Also, we have An overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting according to that Hezbollah rejected the ceasefire, plus Hezbollah himself stated this.
